raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/2024, I spoke with XXXX about the Easy Pay program offered. I paid the Full Balance on my Old Navy/XXXX card in anticipation of making a {$3000.00} purchase after the Credit Limit was increased to {$3000.00}. This purchase was processed effective XX/XX/2024 and was eligible for Easy Pay. ( 18 monthly payments of {$170.00} ) When I tried to enroll in Easy Pay, I received an error message " Oops something went wrong... We were unable to enroll your transaction into Easy Pay because your balance is below the requested enrollment amount. '' When I spoke to XXXX on XX/XX/2024, her response was that the System was updating, try again later. On XX/XX/2024, I spoke to XXXX who transferred me to XXXX, a Supervisor. He was unable to get the transaction to go through. He realized that the Full Amount ( including Interest ) would exceed my available Credit Limit ; hence the denial. On XX/XX/2024, I made a payment of {$500.00} and subsequently attempted to enroll in Easy Pay ( as I was instructed to do by XXXX, a supervisor  I spoke with on XX/XX/2024 ) and the Easy Pay amount of {$2500.00} ( 18 monthly payments of {$140.00} ) would not process, because XXXX had apparently deleted the transaction from the system. I tried to contact the Complaint Department ( Tel. # XXXX ) and the call went directly to " Credit Cards. ''
